title: S-9.2: Dynamic /join/[room] route
description:
## Description

Create a Next.js App Router page that pre-fills room name from URL.

Implementation:
- `src/app/join/[room]/page.tsx` — reads `params.room`, passes to join form
- Or `src/app/join/page.tsx` using `searchParams.room` query param
- Room input pre-filled and disabled when param present
- Room input empty and editable when no param
- Uses `slugifyRoom()` from S-9.1 to normalize param
- Includes SocketProvider wrapper
- Shares avatar picker + username input from existing JoinForm

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

test -f src/app/join/page.tsx || test -f "src/app/join/[room]/page.tsx"
pnpm build

pnpm run dev -- -p 3099 &
PID=$!
sleep 6

playwright-cli open http://localhost:3099/join/test-room
sleep 2

playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('input[disabled]')?.value || document.querySelector('input[readonly]')?.value || ''" > /tmp/pw-join-room.txt 2>&1
grep -qi "test-room" /tmp/pw-join-room.txt

playwright-cli goto http://localhost:3099/join
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelectorAll('input:not([disabled]):not([readonly])').length" > /tmp/pw-join-count.txt 2>&1
python3 -c "assert int(open('/tmp/pw-join-count.txt').read().strip()) >= 2, 'Expected ≥2 editable inputs'"

playwright-cli close
kill $PID 2>/dev/null
rm -f /tmp/pw-join-room.txt /tmp/pw-join-count.txt
```
close_reason: Dynamic /join/[room] route with pre-filled disabled room input, plus /join with editable inputs

title: Epic 9: Shareable Room Links
close_reason: All 4 stories complete: slugify, /join route, invite button, native share

title: Reply/quote messages
description:
## Description

Tap a message to reply to it. Show quoted original message above the reply in the bubble. Thread context without full threading — keeps it simple like iMessage/Telegram replies.

Implementation requires FE + BE changes:
- **types.ts**: add optional `replyTo` field to ChatMessage (id + username + text preview)
- **server.ts**: accept and store `replyTo` in message schema, include in history
- **socket-context.tsx**: pass `replyTo` through sendMessage
- **chat-input.tsx**: reply-to state bar above input showing quoted message, dismiss button
- **message-bubble.tsx**: render quoted original above reply content, tap message to set reply target

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

# Message type includes reply reference
grep -qE "replyTo|reply_to|quotedMessage" src/lib/types.ts

# Server handles reply field in messages
grep -qE "replyTo|reply_to|quotedMessage" server.ts

# Message bubble renders quoted original
grep -qE "reply|quote|Reply|Quote" src/components/message-bubble.tsx

# Chat input has reply-to state/UI
grep -qE "reply|quote|Reply|Quote" src/components/chat-input.tsx

pnpm build

# Functional test: reply UI accessible
pnpm run dev -- -p 3099 &
PID=$!
sleep 6

playwright-cli open http://localhost:3099
sleep 2

# Join and send a message
playwright-cli eval "
  const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('.kid-input');
  if (inputs[0]) { inputs[0].value = 'TestKid'; inputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
  if (inputs[1]) { inputs[1].value = 'reply-test'; inputs[1].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
"
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('.big-button')?.click()"
sleep 2

# Send a message to reply to
playwright-cli eval "
  const input = document.querySelector('textarea') || document.querySelector('input[placeholder]');
  if (input) { input.value = 'Reply to this'; input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
"
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('button[type=\"submit\"]')?.click() || document.querySelector('form button')?.click()"
sleep 2

# Verify messages rendered (reply target exists)
playwright-cli eval "
  const msgs = document.querySelectorAll('[class*=bubble], [class*=message]');
  msgs.length > 0;
" > /tmp/pw-reply.txt 2>&1
grep -q "true" /tmp/pw-reply.txt

pnpm test

playwright-cli close
kill $PID 2>/dev/null
rm -f /tmp/pw-reply.txt
```

title: S-9.3: Copy link / Invite button in chat header
description:
## Description

Add an "Invite" button to the chat room header that copies a shareable join link.

Implementation:
- Add button with `aria-label="Invite"` to chat-room.tsx header
- On click: generate URL `${window.location.origin}/join/${slugifyRoom(room)}`
- Copy to clipboard via `navigator.clipboard.writeText()`
- Show toast "Link copied!" for 2s (simple state-based div, not a library)
- Style as small icon button (link/share icon) fitting kid-friendly design

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

grep -qE "Invite|invite|aria-label.*[Ii]nvite" src/components/chat-room.tsx
pnpm build

pnpm run dev -- -p 3099 &
PID=$!
sleep 6

playwright-cli open http://localhost:3099
sleep 2

# Join a room
playwright-cli eval "
  const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('.kid-input');
  if (inputs[0]) { inputs[0].value = 'TestKid'; inputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
  if (inputs[1]) { inputs[1].value = 'invite-test'; inputs[1].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
"
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('.big-button')?.click()"
sleep 2
playwright-cli snapshot

# Verify invite button exists
playwright-cli eval "!!document.querySelector('[aria-label=\"Invite\"]') || Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button')).some(b => b.textContent.includes('Invite'))" > /tmp/pw-has-invite.txt 2>&1
grep -q "true" /tmp/pw-has-invite.txt

playwright-cli close
kill $PID 2>/dev/null
rm -f /tmp/pw-has-invite.txt
```
close_reason: Invite button with clipboard copy + toast in chat header

title: HTTPS for speech-to-text on LAN devices
description:
## Description

Speech recognition API requires secure context. Chromebook on LAN can't use mic button over plain HTTP.

This is a spike — choose an approach first, then implement.

Options:
1. Cloudflare Tunnel (zero config HTTPS, requires cloudflared)
2. mkcert + local CA (dev-only, install CA on each device)
3. Let's Encrypt + domain (prod-ready, needs public domain)
4. Chrome flag workaround (fragile, per-device)

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Decision documented: which approach chosen and why (in commit message or bead close reason)
2. Implementation complete for chosen approach
3. Dev server accessible over HTTPS on LAN (or secure context equivalent)
4. Chromebook on LAN can activate speech recognition without errors
5. Existing HTTP workflow not broken (HTTPS is additive)

title: S-9.4: Native share API with fallback
description:
## Description

Create a share utility that uses native share API on mobile, clipboard on desktop.

Implementation:
- `src/lib/share.ts` exports `shareRoom(roomSlug: string): Promise<void>`
- If `navigator.share` exists: call with `{ title: "Join my Pig Latin room!", url: <join-url> }`
- If not: fall back to `navigator.clipboard.writeText(url)`
- Wire into the Invite button from S-9.3 (replace direct clipboard call)
- `src/lib/share.test.ts`:
  - Test 1: mock `navigator.share` → verify called with correct payload
  - Test 2: delete `navigator.share` → verify `clipboard.writeText` called with URL
  - Test 3: verify URL format is `<origin>/join/<slug>`

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

test -f src/lib/share.ts
test -f src/lib/share.test.ts
grep -qE "navigator.share|vi.fn|mock" src/lib/share.test.ts

pnpm test -- --run src/lib/share.test.ts
pnpm build
```
close_reason: Native share API with clipboard fallback + 3 tests, wired into Invite button

title: Persist user profile in browser localStorage
description: Save username, avatar, and preferred difficulty to localStorage so users don't have to re-enter name every session. Pre-fill join form from saved profile.

title: Epic 11: Media Messages (Images & GIFs)
description: Support sending images and GIFs in chat. Upload/paste images, GIF picker (Giphy/Tenor), inline preview in message bubbles.

title: S-11.1: Image upload and paste in chat
description: Support pasting or selecting images to send in chat. Store as base64 or upload to local server. Show inline preview in message bubbles.

title: S-11.2: GIF picker integration
description: Add a GIF search button using Tenor/Giphy API. Inline GIF preview in messages. Kid-safe content filter.

title: Epic 10: Avatar & Identity System
description: Improve profile identity in chat — more emoji variety, ability to change avatar mid-chat, richer identity display (Telegram-style).

title: S-10.1: Expand avatar emoji selection
description:
## Description

Current set is 6 animals (bear, cat, dog, fox, owl, bunny). Expand to ~20+ options across categories — animals, mythical creatures, food, space, etc. Update AVATARS array in types.ts.

Must preserve original 6 IDs for backward compat with existing localStorage profiles.

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

# Original 6 IDs preserved (backward compat)
for id in bear cat dog fox owl bunny; do
  grep -q "\"$id\"" src/lib/types.ts
done

# At least 20 entries in AVATARS
AVATAR_COUNT=$(grep -c 'id:' src/lib/types.ts)
test "$AVATAR_COUNT" -ge 20

# Build succeeds (type safety)
pnpm build

# Picker renders all avatars
pnpm run dev -- -p 3099 &
PID=$!
sleep 6

playwright-cli open http://localhost:3099
sleep 2
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelectorAll('[aria-label]').length" > /tmp/pw-avatar-count.txt 2>&1
python3 -c "assert int(open('/tmp/pw-avatar-count.txt').read().strip()) >= 20, 'Expected ≥20 avatar buttons'"

playwright-cli close
kill $PID 2>/dev/null
rm -f /tmp/pw-avatar-count.txt
```

title: S-10.2: Change avatar while in chat room
description:
## Description

Add ability to tap your avatar in the chat header to open a picker and switch mid-conversation. Broadcast the change to other room members so it updates in real-time.

Implementation requires both FE and BE changes:
- **server.ts**: new `change-avatar` socket event — updates `currentUser.avatar` in room map, broadcasts updated `room-users` list
- **socket-context.tsx**: expose `changeAvatar(avatarId)` function, emit `change-avatar` event
- **chat-room.tsx**: clickable avatar in header with `aria-label="Change avatar"`, opens AvatarPicker overlay, calls `changeAvatar` on select
- **profiles.ts**: update localStorage profile when avatar changes

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

# Server handles avatar-change event
grep -qE "change-avatar|update-avatar" server.ts

# Socket context exposes changeAvatar
grep -qE "changeAvatar|updateAvatar" src/lib/socket-context.tsx

# Chat room has clickable avatar trigger in header
grep -qE "Change avatar" src/components/chat-room.tsx

pnpm build

# Functional test: join room, avatar picker accessible
pnpm run dev -- -p 3099 &
PID=$!
sleep 6

playwright-cli open http://localhost:3099
sleep 2

# Join a room
playwright-cli eval "
  const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('.kid-input');
  if (inputs[0]) { inputs[0].value = 'TestKid'; inputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
  if (inputs[1]) { inputs[1].value = 'avatar-test'; inputs[1].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
"
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('.big-button')?.click()"
sleep 2

# Verify clickable avatar element exists in header
playwright-cli eval "!!document.querySelector('[aria-label=\"Change avatar\"]')" > /tmp/pw-avatar-trigger.txt 2>&1
grep -q "true" /tmp/pw-avatar-trigger.txt

# Run all tests to catch sibling regressions
pnpm test

playwright-cli close
kill $PID 2>/dev/null
rm -f /tmp/pw-avatar-trigger.txt
```

title: S-10.3: Richer identity display
description:
## Description

Show avatar + username more prominently in chat — colored name badges, avatar emoji in message bubbles, user profile card on tap. Inspired by Telegram-style identity.

Implementation:
- **message-bubble.tsx**: show avatar emoji + username with colored badge above/beside message content
- Username color derived deterministically from username string (hash → hue)
- Avatar emoji visible in each message bubble (not just header)
- Optional: tap username to see mini profile card (avatar, name, join time)

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

# Avatar emoji rendered in message bubbles
grep -qE "avatar|getAvatarEmoji" src/components/message-bubble.tsx

# Username has colored/styled display
grep -qE "color|bg-|badge|hue|hsl" src/components/message-bubble.tsx

pnpm build

# Visual verification: messages show avatar + styled username
pnpm run dev -- -p 3099 &
PID=$!
sleep 6

playwright-cli open http://localhost:3099
sleep 2

# Join and send a message
playwright-cli eval "
  const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('.kid-input');
  if (inputs[0]) { inputs[0].value = 'TestKid'; inputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
  if (inputs[1]) { inputs[1].value = 'identity-test'; inputs[1].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
"
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('.big-button')?.click()"
sleep 2

# Send a message
playwright-cli eval "
  const input = document.querySelector('input[placeholder]') || document.querySelector('textarea');
  if (input) { input.value = 'Hello world'; input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
"
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('button[type=\"submit\"]')?.click() || document.querySelector('form button')?.click()"
sleep 2

# Verify message area has rendered content
playwright-cli eval "
  const msgs = document.querySelectorAll('[class*=message], [class*=bubble]');
  msgs.length > 0;
" > /tmp/pw-identity.txt 2>&1
grep -q "true" /tmp/pw-identity.txt

# Run all tests to catch sibling regressions
pnpm test

playwright-cli close
kill $PID 2>/dev/null
rm -f /tmp/pw-identity.txt
```

title: S-9.1: Room name slugification utility
description:
## Description

Create `src/lib/slugify.ts` exporting `slugifyRoom(name: string): string`.

Rules:
- Lowercase, trim whitespace
- Replace spaces with hyphens
- Strip non-alphanumeric chars (except hyphens)
- Strip emoji (Unicode > U+FFFF)
- Collapse multiple consecutive hyphens
- Idempotent (slugifying a slug returns same slug)
- Throw on empty/whitespace-only input

Create `src/lib/slugify.test.ts` with ≥ 8 test cases:
- `"Rainbow Party!"` → `"rainbow-party"`
- `"hello world"` → `"hello-world"`
- `"  spaces  "` → `"spaces"`
- `"UPPERCASE"` → `"uppercase"`
- `"special@#chars!"` → `"specialchars"`
- `"emoji 🎉 room"` → `"emoji-room"`
- `"already-slugged"` → `"already-slugged"`
- `""` → throws

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

test -f src/lib/slugify.ts
test -f src/lib/slugify.test.ts
grep -qE "expect\(.*\)\." src/lib/slugify.test.ts

pnpm test -- --run src/lib/slugify.test.ts
pnpm build
```
close_reason: Slugify utility + 10 test cases, all passing

title: Multiline messages with markdown/rich text support
description:
## Description

Support multiline messages and basic markdown rendering (bold, italic, links) in chat bubbles. Textarea instead of single-line input.

Implementation:
- **chat-input.tsx**: replace `<input type="text">` with `<textarea>`, Enter sends (Shift+Enter for newline)
- **message-bubble.tsx**: render markdown (bold, italic, links) — use lightweight renderer, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization
- **server.ts**: no changes needed (newlines already preserved as strings)
- XSS safety: no `javascript:` links, sanitize any HTML output

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

# Input is textarea (not single-line input)
grep -qE "textarea|TextArea" src/components/chat-input.tsx

# Markdown rendering in message bubbles
grep -qE "markdown|bold|italic|react-markdown|dangerouslySetInnerHTML" src/components/message-bubble.tsx

# No dangerous innerHTML without sanitization
! grep -q "dangerouslySetInnerHTML" src/components/message-bubble.tsx || \
  grep -qE "sanitize|DOMPurify|xss" src/components/message-bubble.tsx

pnpm build

# Functional test: multiline message renders
pnpm run dev -- -p 3099 &
PID=$!
sleep 6

playwright-cli open http://localhost:3099
sleep 2

# Join a room
playwright-cli eval "
  const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('.kid-input');
  if (inputs[0]) { inputs[0].value = 'TestKid'; inputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
  if (inputs[1]) { inputs[1].value = 'md-test'; inputs[1].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
"
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('.big-button')?.click()"
sleep 2

# Verify textarea exists (not input[type=text])
playwright-cli eval "!!document.querySelector('textarea')" > /tmp/pw-textarea.txt 2>&1
grep -q "true" /tmp/pw-textarea.txt

pnpm test

playwright-cli close
kill $PID 2>/dev/null
rm -f /tmp/pw-textarea.txt
```

title: Emoji reactions on messages
description:
## Description

Tap/long-press a message to add emoji reactions (thumbs up, heart, laugh, etc). Show reaction counts under the message bubble. Broadcast reactions to all room members in real-time.

Implementation requires FE + BE changes:
- **types.ts**: add `reactions` field to ChatMessage (map of emoji → array of usernames)
- **server.ts**: new `add-reaction` / `remove-reaction` socket events, store reactions in memory + DB, broadcast updated reactions to room
- **socket-context.tsx**: expose `addReaction(messageId, emoji)` / `removeReaction(messageId, emoji)`
- **message-bubble.tsx**: reaction bar below message content showing emoji + count, tap to toggle own reaction, long-press/tap to open emoji picker

## Exit Condition

```bash
set -e
cd /Users/thom/10-19_projects/upidstay

# Server handles reaction events
grep -qE "add-reaction|remove-reaction|react" server.ts

# Socket context exposes reaction functions
grep -qE "addReaction|removeReaction|sendReaction" src/lib/socket-context.tsx

# Message bubble shows reaction counts
grep -qE "reaction|Reaction" src/components/message-bubble.tsx

# Reaction data in message type
grep -qE "reactions" src/lib/types.ts

pnpm build

# Functional test: reaction UI accessible
pnpm run dev -- -p 3099 &
PID=$!
sleep 6

playwright-cli open http://localhost:3099
sleep 2

# Join and send a message
playwright-cli eval "
  const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('.kid-input');
  if (inputs[0]) { inputs[0].value = 'TestKid'; inputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
  if (inputs[1]) { inputs[1].value = 'react-test'; inputs[1].dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
"
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('.big-button')?.click()"
sleep 2

# Send a message to react to
playwright-cli eval "
  const input = document.querySelector('textarea') || document.querySelector('input[placeholder]');
  if (input) { input.value = 'React to me'; input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles:true})); }
"
sleep 1
playwright-cli eval "document.querySelector('button[type=\"submit\"]')?.click() || document.querySelector('form button')?.click()"
sleep 2

# Verify messages rendered
playwright-cli eval "
  const msgs = document.querySelectorAll('[class*=bubble], [class*=message]');
  msgs.length > 0;
" > /tmp/pw-reactions.txt 2>&1
grep -q "true" /tmp/pw-reactions.txt

pnpm test

playwright-cli close
kill $PID 2>/dev/null
rm -f /tmp/pw-reactions.txt
```
